task_completion.description: The agent opens the provided baseline script to begin configuring the mission.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT application open with lunar_baseline.script loaded, or a text editor showing the contents of ~/Documents/missions/lunar_baseline.script.

task_completion.description: The agent configures the orbit propagation to last for at least 3 days starting from March 13.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT GUI showing a Propagate command configured for at least 3 days (e.g., ElapsedDays = 3), or the script text modified to reflect this propagation time and epoch.

task_completion.description: The agent configures an Eclipse Locator to track occultations by Earth and Luna and output a report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT EclipseLocator properties window open showing OccultingBodies set to Earth and Luna, and the output file path set to ~/GMAT_output/EclipseReport.txt, or equivalent script code.

task_completion.description: The agent runs the simulation and successfully generates the eclipse report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: GMAT mission run completing successfully, or a terminal/file explorer/text editor showing the newly created EclipseReport.txt in the ~/GMAT_output/ directory.

task_completion.description: The agent creates the JSON file containing the maximum umbra duration.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing the creation and contents of ~/GMAT_output/eclipse_analysis.json with the exact key 'max_umbra_duration_seconds' and a numerical value (expected to be > 3500.0).

integrity.description: The agent actually ran the GMAT simulation rather than manually typing a fake report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the GMAT GUI actively running the mission, or terminal commands executing the GMAT binary to process the script.

integrity.description: The agent extracted the maximum duration value from the generated report rather than hallucinating it.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent viewing the contents of EclipseReport.txt on screen to identify the longest continuous Umbra duration before writing it to the JSON file.

integrity.description: The agent placed the output files in the exact requested directories.
integrity.visual_evidence: File paths in the terminal or text editor explicitly showing /home/ga/GMAT_output/ for both EclipseReport.txt and eclipse_analysis.json.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The baseline script is located at /home/ga/Documents/missions/lunar_baseline.script. The eclipse report must be saved to /home/ga/GMAT_output/EclipseReport.txt. The final JSON output must be saved to /home/ga/GMAT_output/eclipse_analysis.json. The correct maximum umbra duration is expected to be at least 3500.0 seconds (typically around 3900 seconds for the March 14, 2025 total lunar eclipse).
